content.text:
Question:
                                I've got some free time tonight, any documentary recommendations?

                                Question date:
                                2023/05/22 (Mon) 01:48

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                The user would prefer documentary recommendations that are similar in style and theme to 'Our Planet', 'Free Solo', and 'Tiger King', which they have previously enjoyed. They might not prefer recommendations of documentaries that are vastly different in tone or subject matter from these titles. The preferred response utilizes the user's previously mentioned viewing history to suggest documentaries that cater to their tastes.

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            If the user asks about an initial, previous, older, or otherwise historical state, non-current typed memory and older evidence may be the direct answer; compare it with current active memory instead of blindly preferring the current value.
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:b0479f84
            Context mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: MINIMAL
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-dc974e67b4ad-claim-5 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has watched the documentary "Free Solo".; scope=User's documentary viewing history from the imported 2023-05-21 chat session.; context="The user asked for documentary recommendations similar to "Our Planet", "Free Solo", and "Tiger King", which they had just finished."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:0543dffbf88715d763813cfdc00de84e quote="similar to "Our Planet", "Free Solo", and "Tiger King", which I just finished"
- claim hot-path-dc974e67b4ad-claim-4 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has watched the documentary series "Our Planet".; scope=User's documentary viewing history from the imported 2023-05-21 chat session.; context="The user asked for documentary recommendations similar to "Our Planet", "Free Solo", and "Tiger King", which they had just finished."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:0543dffbf88715d763813cfdc00de84e quote="similar to "Our Planet", "Free Solo", and "Tiger King", which I just finished"
- claim hot-path-dc974e67b4ad-claim-6 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has watched the documentary series "Tiger King".; scope=User's documentary viewing history from the imported 2023-05-21 chat session.; context="The user asked for documentary recommendations similar to "Our Planet", "Free Solo", and "Tiger King", which they had just finished."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:0543dffbf88715d763813cfdc00de84e quote="similar to "Our Planet", "Free Solo", and "Tiger King", which I just finished"
- claim hot-path-dc974e67b4ad-claim-7 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user prefers watching documentaries on Netflix.; scope=User's documentary platform preference from the imported 2023-05-21 chat session.; context="The user said they had been watching many documentaries lately, especially on Netflix, while asking for more documentary recommendations."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:0543dffbf88715d763813cfdc00de84e quote="I've been watching a lot of documentaries lately, especially on Netflix."
- claim hot-path-dc974e67b4ad-claim-8 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user is interested in coral reefs.; scope=User's nature and conservation interests from the imported 2023-05-21 chat session.; context="The user said they had seen coral reef footage before but had never really understood the importance of coral reefs, and asked why coral reefs are crucial to the ocean ecosystem."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:0543dffbf88715d763813cfdc00de84e quote="I've seen some stunning footage of coral reefs before, but I've never really understood the importance of them."

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-dc974e67b4ad-note-2 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: Documentary-to-coral-reef interest trajectory; In the 2023-05-21 session, the user moved from Netflix documentary recommendations into a focused interest in nature and wildlife documentaries, especially Chasing Coral, coral reef importance, reef protection, eco-tourism, and Belize as a potential responsible-travel destination.; scope=User's reusable recommendation and travel-interest context from LongMemEval b0479f84 session 1; evidence=SUMMARIZED source=external:provided-text:0543dffbf88715d763813cfdc00de84e quote="I'm interested in learning more about eco-tourism."

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:0543dffbf88715d763813cfdc00de84e [external_record ref=longmemeval:b0479f84:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/21 (Sun) 18:47
Transcript:
user: I've been watching a lot of documentaries lately, especially on Netflix. Can you recommend some more documentary series similar to "Our Planet", "Free Solo", and "Tiger King", which I just finished?
assistant: Great taste! Those are all amazing documentaries! Here are some more documentary series on Netflix and other platforms that you might enjoy:...
...3. **March of the Penguins** (Netflix) - A heartwarming documentary about Emperor Penguins in Antarctica.
4. **Dynasties** (Netflix) - Follow the lives of five animal families in the wild, narrated by David Attenborough.
5. **Chasing Coral** (Netflix) - A visually stunning documentary about the decline of coral reefs.

**Adventure and Exploration:**

1. **The Last Lions** (Netflix) - A gripping documentary about the decline of lions in Africa.
2. **The Cave** (Netflix) - A harrowing documentary about a group of women who risk their lives to save others in war-torn Syria....
...3. **The Staircase** (Netflix) - A classic true-crime documentary series about a murder trial....
...**Other Recommendations:**

1. **The Inventor: Out for Blood in Silicon Valley** (HBO Max) - The rise and fall of Theranos, a biotech company.
2. **Fyre: The Greatest Party That Never Happened** (Netflix) - The infamous failed music festival documentary.
3. **The Game Changers** (Netflix) - A documentary about the benefits of a plant-based diet.
4. **The Family I Had** (Netflix) - A heartwarming documentary about a woman who discovers her biological family.
5. **The Imposter** (Amazon Prime) - A true story about a young Frenchman who impersonated a missing Texas boy.

Enjoy your next documentary binge!
user: I'm interested in the nature and wildlife documentaries. Can you tell me more about "Dynasties" and "Chasing Coral"? What makes them unique compared to "Our Planet" and "Planet Earth"?...
...* **Intimate, character-driven storytelling**: The series follows specific individuals and their families, showcasing their struggles, triumphs, and relationships. This approach creates an emotional connection with the audience, making the documentary feel more like a drama series....
...* **A personal, urgent story**: "Chasing Coral" is a more personal and urgent documentary than "Our Planet" or "Planet Earth". The film follows a team of divers, photographers, and scientists as they document the decline of coral reefs and the devastating impact of climate change.
* **Cinematic, immersive underwater footage**: The documentary features breathtaking, high-definition footage of coral reefs, showcasing their beauty and importance. The cinematography is stunning, making you feel like you're swimming alongside the divers.
* **A call to action**: "Chasing Coral" is not just a documentary; it's a passionate plea to raise awareness about the coral reef crisis. The filmmakers hope to inspire viewers to take action and make a difference....
...user: I've seen some stunning footage of coral reefs before, but I've never really understood the importance of them. Can you tell me more about why coral reefs are so crucial to the ocean's ecosystem?...
...user: That's really interesting. So, what are some ways that we can help protect coral reefs?...
...user: I'm interested in learning more about eco-tourism. Can you recommend some popular destinations for eco-tourism and responsible travel that focus on coral reef conservation?...
...user: I'll choose a destination from your list. How about Belize? I've heard great things about the snorkeling and diving there. Can you tell me more about the eco-tourism opportunities in Belize?...
...* Resorts like Victoria House Resort and Chabil Mar Resort offer luxurious amenities while prioritizing environmental sustainability....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [PRODUCT]: Chasing Coral; Product named Chasing Coral.
- entity entity:<1> [PRODUCT]: Dynasties; Product named Dynasties.
- entity entity:<2> [PRODUCT]: Free Solo; Product named Free Solo.
- entity entity:<3> [PRODUCT]: Our Planet; Product named Our Planet.
- entity entity:<4> [SERVICE]: Netflix; Service named Netflix.
- entity entity:<5> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

structured.fullText: {"hypothesis":"Recommend documentaries aligned with the user's prior Netflix documentary tastes, especially similar to Our Planet, Free Solo, and Tiger King, with nature/wildlife options like Dynasties or Chasing Coral fitting their expressed interests.","reason":"The memory directly states the user had just finished Our Planet, Free Solo, and Tiger King, watches documentaries especially on Netflix, and showed follow-up interest in nature/wildlife documentaries.","supported":true}
